layer_height:
0.20 mm first-pass fit-check; if dimensions are confirmed and surface finish is acceptable, keep 0.20 mm for functional prototype. Use adaptive layers only if your slicer profile benefits from it.

Infill: 35% gyroid for fit-check/functional prototype; increase to 45–50% if the bracket sees sustained bending loads. Use 4–5 walls/perimeters before increasing infill.

Supports: Minimal to none if geometry allows; use tree/organic supports only for overhangs that affect fit. Keep support contact away from critical load surfaces and mating faces.

Handoff Notes:
- Start with fit-check settings before structural settings.
- Orient the bracket so the primary bend/load path is not relying only on layer adhesion; place the strongest section to minimize peel across layers.
- Prioritize part orientation that protects bend strength while preserving mating dimensions for the bench fit.
- PETG-CF on Bambu X1C: dry filament well, use a hardened nozzle, and verify extrusion stability before committing to the full part.
- Verify critical holes, slot widths, and mounting interfaces on the first print before increasing load or infill.

layer_height:
0.20 mm for fit-check first; if surfaces look acceptable and time allows, keep at 0.20 mm for final functional prototype.

Infill: 35% gyroid for fit-check, then 40–50% gyroid for load-oriented print if the bracket is confirmed to fit.

Supports: Use only if needed for overhangs; prefer tree/organic supports with conservative interface. Avoid supports on critical mating or bend regions. Orient the part so the main bend/load path is not relying only on Z-layer adhesion.

Handoff Notes: Start with fit-check settings before structural settings. For the Bambu X1C with PETG-CF, dry filament first and use a hardened nozzle. Verify bracket fit with a low-risk prototype before load testing. Orient the part to maximize bend strength, keeping the highest-stress section in-plane where possible. If the fit-check is successful, increase infill and maintain the same orientation rather than rotating the part.

layer_height:
0.20 mm for initial fit-check; if geometry is forgiving, 0.24 mm is acceptable for a faster functional prototype.
Infill: 35% gyroid for fit-check, then 45% gyroid if the bracket will see real loading.
Supports: Use only where needed; prefer organic/tree or snug supports on overhangs. Orient the bracket so the main bend/load path is not dependent on layer adhesion alone, even if that means a small support penalty.
Handoff Notes: Start with fit-check settings before structural settings. Print one bracket first to verify hole alignment, mating surfaces, and clearances before load testing. For PETG-CF on the X1C, keep cooling moderate and dry filament well. If the part has a defined bend line, rotate it so the bend is supported by perimeter continuity rather than pure Z-layer strength.
